---
title: "prexorctl module install"
description: "Install a platform module"
---

<!-- auto-generated by tools/gen-cli-docs.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

Install a platform module.
Two sources, auto-detected from the argument:
  • Local bundle — a .jar (sidecar auto-detected as <jar>.cosign.bundle or
    <jar>.sig, or via --signature) or a .tar/.tar.gz/.tgz containing one jar
    and one sidecar. The signature is uploaded so the controller can verify
    it before installing.
  • Registry — a module spec like "stats-aggregator" or
    "stats-aggregator@1.2.0". The controller pulls the signed jar from a
    configured registry (modules.registries), checks its sha256 against the
    index, and verifies the signature against its own trust root. Omit the
    version (or use @latest) for the newest. Use --registry to pin one of the
    configured registries.

## Usage

```text
prexorctl module install <jar|bundle.tar | id[@version]> [flags]
```

## Flags

```text
--check-requires     Before uploading, read module.yaml from the jar and warn for any required capability that no installed controller module provides.
    -h, --help               help for install
        --registry string    For a registry install (id[@version]), pin one of the configured registry URLs instead of searching all of them.
        --signature string   Explicit path to the signature sidecar (.sig or .cosign.bundle). Defaults to autodetection of <jar>.cosign.bundle or <jar>.sig.
```

## Global flags

```text
--ascii               Use ASCII glyphs only (no unicode box drawing, sparklines, etc.)
        --context string      Override active context for this invocation
    -c, --controller string   Override controller URL
    -j, --json                Output in JSON format
        --no-color            Disable colored output
    -t, --token string        Override auth token
    -v, --verbose             Show HTTP request/response details
```
